user.title: Aruba IAP Device Tracker
user.description:
Enter the connection details for your Aruba Instant AP.

Make sure REST API is enabled on the IAP:
`(config)# allow-rest-api` then `# commit apply`

data_description.host: IP address of your Aruba IAP or Virtual Controller (e.g. 192.168.1.1)

tracking.title: Tracking & Polling
tracking.description:
**Track new devices by default?**

- **On** — new devices are automatically tracked as soon as they are discovered.
- **Off** — new devices are discovered but their tracker entities will be **disabled** in HA. You can enable them individually in Settings → Devices & Services.

Both settings can be changed at any time.

data_description.scan_interval: How often to query the IAP for connected clients. Min 10s, max 300s.

error.cannot_connect: Cannot reach the Aruba IAP. Check the IP address, confirm the device is online, and that REST API is enabled with 'allow-rest-api'.
error.invalid_auth: Login failed. Check the username and password.
error.api_access_denied: Login succeeded but the account cannot run API commands. Ensure the user has full management privileges, not read-only.
error.unknown: Unexpected error. Check the Home Assistant logs for details.

abort.already_configured: This Aruba IAP is already configured.

init.title: Aruba IAP Settings
init.description:
Update connection details or change tracking and polling preferences.

If you change the IP address, username, or password the connection will be tested before saving.

data_description.track_new_devices: When off, new devices are discovered but their entities are disabled until you enable them manually.
data_description.scan_interval: How often to query the IAP for connected clients. Min 10s, max 300s.

error.cannot_connect: Cannot reach the Aruba IAP at the new address. Check the IP and try again.
error.invalid_auth: Login failed with the new credentials.
error.api_access_denied: Login succeeded but the account cannot run API commands. Use a full-privilege management account.
error.unknown: Unexpected error. Check the Home Assistant logs.
